business.headline: Sensex falls 1,000 points: Why is the stock market falling today?The Sensex fell over 1,000 points to 74,272.68 and the Nifty 50 dropped nearly 300 points to 23,009 in early trade, even as US President Donald Trump signalled a pause in military escalation against Iran.

Will petrol, diesel prices fall after government's excise duty cut?The government has sharply reduced excise duty on petrol and diesel. Does this mean retail fuel prices will fall? Here's all you need to know.

Rupee at record low as it slips past 94 mark against US dollarRupee dropped to 94.1575 per dollar during the day. This is lower than its previous all-time low of 93.98, which was recorded earlier this week.
Sensex, Nifty fall over 1% as West Asia war wages on for 4th weekThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 1,000.77 points to 74,272.68, while the NSE Nifty50 tanked 299.45 points to 23,007.00 as of 9:38 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Stock markets to rise or fall today as Iran war continues?Early signals from the derivatives market suggest a weak start. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,144 at around 8:01 am. This points to a likely opening below the previous close of 23,306.45 on the Nifty 50.

Government cuts excise duty by Rs 10 on petrol and diesel amid West Asia warThe excise duty on petrol has been cut to Rs 3 per litre from Rs 13 per litre earlier. This marks a reduction of Rs 10 per litre. Excise duty on diesel has been brought down to Rs 0 per litre from Rs 10 per litre earlier, also a cut of Rs 10 per litre.

Don't miss these 3 financial tasks before March 31, 2026With just days left for the financial year to close, it's time to act. A few pending tasks could impact your taxes and paperwork. Here are three things you should finish before March 31, 2026.

Saudi oil flows to India and China likely to dip next month: ReportFor April, exports to India are estimated at around 23 million barrels. This is slightly lower compared to recent months, when shipments ranged between 25 million and 28 million barrels, according to data from Kpler Ltd and Vortexa Ltd.

Nearly 2 months of fuel available, next 2 months also secured: GovernmentThe Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas said crude oil supplies have already been secured for the next 60 days. Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs) have tied up imports in advance, ensuring there is no gap in supply.

Petrol, diesel costlier: Nayara raises prices by up to Rs 5 per litrePublic sector oil marketing companies (OMCs) such as Indian Oil and Hindustan Petroleum have raised premium petrol prices by 2.09 to 2.35 per litre since March 20.

Think you're investing right? These 3 habits may be costing you big moneyYou don't need a market crash to lose money. Sometimes, it's the small, repeated investing habits that do the real damage—without you even noticing. Let's find out.

Will Dalal Street's recovery run end as US, Iran fail to end war?With domestic markets shut today, Friday's opening becomes the first real test of whether the recent rally can hold as investors react to fresh geopolitical developments and global market cues.

NSE, BSE holiday: Is stock market open or closed today for Ram Navami?The Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and the National Stock Exchange (NSE) will remain closed on March 26 due to Ram Navami. There will be no trading activity across segments on both exchanges for the day.

Government retains 4% retail inflation target till 2031The central government has retained the 4% retail inflation target with a 2–6% tolerance band for another five years, from April 2026 to March 2031, in consultation with the RBI.

Google, Meta found liable for $3 million in damages in online addiction suitThe lawsuit sought USD 3 million in damages. The outcome could influence thousands of similar cases against tech companies brought by parents, attorneys general and school districts.

In a 1st since 2019, India buys Iran LPG cargo as US eases sanctions, claims reportIndia had shunned energy purchases from Iran in 2019 under pressure from Western sanctions. The tanker was initially bound for China, according to LSEG data.

Free gas up to Rs 500, zero security fee: Govt pushes PNG shift for householdsThe government's focus remains on maintaining supply and reducing dependence on imports, while ensuring households have access to reliable and affordable energy.

8th Pay Commission timeline out: Here's what employees can expect nextIf you've been waiting for clarity on the 8th Pay Commission, there's finally some movement. The government has shared a timeline, giving employees a better idea of when changes in salaries and pensions could come.

entertainment.headline: Mandana Karimi calls out Boman Irani's Iran satire: Interesting timing Mandana Karimi has criticised Boman Irani for his satire on Trump-Iran peace talks, calling out the hypocrisy of speaking now while ignoring years of Iranian suffering.

Mammootty to Mohanlal: South cinema's OGs who refuse to slow down Veteran actors in South cinema are defying retirement rumours by consistently delivering diverse and content-driven films. Their collaborations across generations keep the industry vibrant and audiences engaged.

Singer Saapna Mukerji praises Aditya Dhar for keeping her classic song Oye Oye alive Singer Saapna Mukerji was thrilled to see her classic song Oye Oye become popular again through Dhurandhar 2. She said it is special that Gen-Z is enjoying it and praised Aditya Dhar for keeping the song's original charm alive.

Anurag Dobhal's wife Ritika says stress affecting unborn child, denies his claims Anurag Dobhal's wife, Ritika Chauhan, broke her silence after his car crash and allegations against in-laws. She hinted at deeper tensions within the family. Her emotional note raised questions about what really went wrong.

Scene-stealer at 20: Why Dhurandhar's Sara Arjun is the heart of the film Sara Arjun has captivated audiences with her nuanced portrayal of Yalina Jamali in Dhurandhar: The Revenge. Her restrained acting and expressive eyes bring depth to a complex character amid intense action and drama despite limited footage.

Meryl Streep praises Anne Hathaway's stand on body image in Devil Wears Prada 2 Meryl Streep praised Anne Hathaway for taking a stand on body image on The Devil Wears Prada 2 sets. She urged producers not to cast 'thin models' and promote healthier portrayals.

Rakesh Bedi, Arjun Rampal, Gaurav Gera: How Dhurandhar reinvents familiar faces What's really heartening about the Dhurandhar franchise is how it's quietly giving a second wind to several seasoned actors who had been pushed to the sidelines in recent years. Instead of relying only on fresh faces or big stars, the films have smartly tapped into experienced talent.

The End of Oak Street trailer: Anne Hathaway, Ewan McGregor lead eerie sci-fi drama The trailer of The End of Oak Street, starring Anne Hathaway and Ewan McGregor, offers a glimpse into a mysterious sci-fi story set in a strange new world. The film will release in August this year.

Dhurandhar 2's Udaybir Sandhu says working with Ranveer Singh is 'a cakewalk' Actor Udaybir Sandhu opened up about his experience working on Dhurandhar 2. He praised Ranveer Singh and Aditya Dhar for creating a supportive and creatively free environment on set.

Ranveer Singh-Deepika's casual dosa date with his father Jagjit, pic goes viral Deepika Padukone and Ranveer Singh posed for photos with restaurant staff during a Mumbai outing, joined by Singh's father, Jagjit Singh Bhavnani. The rare public appearance quickly went viral.

Rajpal Yadav back with Akshay Kumar after 11 years in Bhooth Bangla: Same hunger Rajpal Yadav discusses Bhooth Bangla, horror comedy and his evolving philosophy as an actor. He also reflects on how social media aided resurgence among younger audiences.

Aakhri Sawal: Sanjay Dutt to act in and produce film about 100 years of RSS Sanjay Dutt's Aakhri Sawal is based on the 100-year-old journey of RSS (Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh). He has acted in and produced the upcoming film based on the pre-independence era.

Daredevil Born Again 2, Episode 1 First Impression: Raw action, subtle romance Charlie Cox's Matt Murdock, or Daredevil, returns to Hell's Kitchen with a fierce battle against Vincent D'Onofrio's Wilson Fisk aka Kingpin. Deborah Ann Woll's evolved Karen reignites old flames in a pulse-pounding premiere that blends brutal action with subtle intimacy.

Deepak Tijori questions censor policy over half-muted abuses in Dhurandhar 2 Deepak Tijori has raised questions over 'half-muted' abuses in Dhurandhar 2, sparking a debate on censorship and OTT releases.

He did things better than me: Priyadarshan on ex-assistant Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar Filmmaker Priyadarshan spoke about Dhurandhar director Aditya Dhar, who assisted him on films such as Aakrosh and Tezz. The veteran director, who is gearing up for the Akshay Kumar-starrer Bhooth Bangla, also opened up about release-day anxiety and creative conviction.

I was awestruck: Samyuktha on Tabu, Vijay Sethupathi, Puri Jagannadh Actor Samyuktha is excited about Slumdog: 33 Temple Road, praising Tabu and her easy collaboration with Vijay Sethupathi under Puri Jagannadh. The pan-Indian action thriller is set for a June release.

Trump signature to appear on all new US currency in historic shiftThe move also breaks with a 165-year tradition by removing the Treasurer of the United States' signature from federal currency for the first time since the system began in 1861.

Amid the US' war with Iran, let's take a look back at one of America's most humiliating defeats, in which the force it backed surrendered within 72 hours.

Trump extends Iran pause but US military build-up tells another storyDonald Trump has extended the pause in attacking Iran's energy facilities by 10 days. Such a large-hearted gesture by Trump has not only taken Iran by surprise, but has, at the same time, made it sceptical whether it was a ruse to buy time to amass thousands of additional troops near Tehran. However, several experts have predicted Saturday to be D-day, with a high likelihood that the US will put boots on the ground.

Live: 6 killed in Iran's Qom as US-Israel launch joint strikesUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The Middle East conflict showed no signs of easing as fresh strikes were reported in Iran, Israel and Lebanon. Israel struck key infrastructure at multiple locations inside Iran, while Iranian missiles targeted not just Israel but also the UAE. At least two people were killed in the UAE from the debris of an intercepted missile. Israel also claimed it killed Alireza Tangsiri, the commander of Iran's IRGC navy, in a strike. Israel also intensified operations in Lebanon. On the diplomatic front, US President Donald Trump said Iran's leaders are begging to make a deal, while warning Washington could escalate further if needed. He also halted strikes on Iran's energy sites until April 6. The US has put forward a 15-point peace plan, with Pakistan acting as a mediator, but Iran has rejected the proposal as "one-sided and unfair". Follow all the latest updates on the ongoing Middle East conflict on indiatoday.in.

Iran strikes leave 13 US bases uninhabitable, troops forced into remote warWith key bases damaged or unsafe, thousands of US troops have been scattered across the region. Some have been moved as far as Europe, while others remain in the Middle East but no longer operate from their original bases.

sports.headline: Italy stay alive in FIFA World Cup 2026 race, Gyokeres hat-trick boosts Sweden Italy moved one step closer to ending their World Cup drought with a 2-0 win over Northern Ireland. Meanwhile, Viktor Gyokeres' hat-trick powered Sweden into the playoff final, setting up a decisive clash against Poland.

Dhoni shouldn't play IPL if he has to remain on CSK's Impact Sub list: Ashwin Former India off-spinner Ravichandran Ashwin has firmly stated that MS Dhoni should not be used as an Impact Player for Chennai Super Kings in IPL 2026. Ashwin insisted that if Dhoni is part of the squad, he must be included in the playing XI rather than being a substitute.

RCB's Nuwan Thushara likely to miss IPL 2026 after Sri Lanka Cricket denies NOC Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) are set to miss the services of overseas pacer Nuwan Thushara after Sri Lanka Cricket reportedly denied him a No Objection Certificate (NOC) for Indian Premier League 2026.

RCB handed timely boost with Josh Hazlewood joining squad before IPL opener RCB's IPL 2026 build-up gets a boost with Josh Hazlewood joining the squad ahead of the opener. However, fitness concerns mean the Australian pacer is likely to miss the early games, testing their bowling depth.

IPL 2026: Lucknow Super Giants will have a chance to make amends in the upcoming season as they chase their maiden title. Rishabh Pant, too, will have a point to prove after a disappointing 2025 campaign, where he struggled both as captain and with the bat.

Ancelotti's Brazil fail first big test, lose to 10-man France before World Cup Brazil's World Cup build-up hit an early bump as Carlo Ancelotti's side lost to 10-man France in a key friendly. Having found a rebirth in his faith, Pyfer has no fear going up against one of the best in the history of the middleweight division.

Pink ball T20? Hyderabad Kingsmen trolled after ball colour changes in PSL opener PSL 2026: Hyderabad Kingsmen were trolled after the ball turned pink during their opener against Lahore Qalandars on Thursday, due to dye from their jerseys.

Carolina Marin announces retirement, cites persistent injury battles as reasonCarolina Marin has announced her retirement from badminton, citing persistent injuries as the reason behind the call on Thursday, March 26. Marin calls time on her career as an Olympic champion and with three world championship titles to his name.

Max Verstappen kicks out English journalist before Japanese GP media sessionRed Bull star Max Verstappen reignites tensions with a British reporter, halting his Suzuka media session before the Japanese Grand Prix. The former World Champion kicked out the journalist for his question last year at the Abu Dhabi Grand Prix.

Are teams prioritising second home? RCB vs MI in Raipur tells a storyIPL 2026: As the BCCI unveiled the full league schedule, it became clear that second homes are now being used not just as backup bases, but as platforms for high-profile clashes and wider fan outreach.

Don't have to prove my worth in IPL: Gill unfazed despite World Cup snubIPL 2026: India's Test and ODI captain Shubman Gill said he is not viewing the upcoming IPL season as a platform to prove his worth in T20Is following his omission from the World Cup squad. Gill was speaking to the press in Ahmedabad on Thursday, 26 March.

Transgender athletes banned from women's sports at Olympics under new policyTransgender athletes have been banned from competing at the Olympics after a new policy on female eligibility was passed by the International Olympic Committee (IOC) on Thursday, March 26.

Virat Kohli adds new layer to his tattoos ahead of RCB's IPL 2026 title defenceAhead of the new IPL 2026 season, Virat Kohli has added a new layer to his tattoo collection on his left arm. Kohli has added a cohesive sleeve on his left arm, which reintegrates his previous ones and restores them.

Will 19th time be the charm for Shreyas Iyer's Punjab Kings?Punjab Kings head into IPL 2026 with a well-balanced and deeper squad under skipper Shreyas Iyer and head coach Ricky Ponting, carrying strong momentum after last season's runner-up finish.

BCCI announces IPL 2026 2nd phase schedule, 13 venues to host 70 league matchesIPL 2026: A total of 70 league matches will be played across 13 venues between 28 March and 24 May. Defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru will not play all their home matches in Bengaluru for the first time, as they are set to travel to Raipur for two games in May. The venues and dates for the playoffs and the final will be announced at a later stage.

Rodri denies feud with Vinicius Jr, open to La Liga transferManchester City midfielder Rodri denies any rift with Vinícius Junior after the Ballon d'Or controversy and hints at a possible move to Madrid, potentially in La Liga, as his Manchester City contract nears expiry.

Chennai, Ahmedabad among venues to host Border-Gavaskar Trophy 2027India will host Australia in the much-anticipated Border-Gavaskar Trophy, with the five-Test series beginning on January 21, 2027, in Nagpur.

More ODIs for Virat and Rohit as BCCI announces 2026-27 home season scheduleThe BCCI announced the schedule for the senior men's team's 2026-27 home season on Thursday, 26 March. A total of nine ODIs will be played in this period, offering fans ample opportunities to watch Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma in action.

Senegal approaches CAS after controversially being stripped of AFCON titleSenegal have approached the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS) after being stripped of their Africa Cup of Nations title by CAF. The governing body controversially awarded the crown to Morocco following a chaotic final. The appeal could take months, with no clear timeline yet set for a verdict.

IPL 2026: How Rishabh Pant trained under Yuvraj Singh in pursuit of LSG redemptionFormer India cricketer Irfan Pathan has shed light on the intense preparation Rishabh Pant is undergoing with veteran Yuvraj Singh ahead of the crucial Indian Premier League 2026 season.

Kylian Mbappe denies that Real Madrid examined the wrong knee after injuryKylian Mbappe has denied reports that his knee injury was misdiagnosed after Real Madrid's medical staff allegedly examined the wrong knee while attempting to treat the issue.

In a scathing tweet, Modi called out Goenka and told him to jog his memory.

IPL 2026: Pat Cummins targets mid-season return for SRH after back injuryIPL 2026: Sunrisers Hyderabad captain Pat Cummins has said that his best-case scenario is to return to full fitness for the second half of the IPL, including the knockout stages in late May.

Gujarat Titans Preview: About time Shubman Gill steps up as captainIPL 2026: Shubman Gill has been exceptional with the bat for Gujarat Titans, but he is still searching for a defining impact as captain. This season, the big question is whether he can lead the Titans to a second IPL title - their first since Hardik Pandya guided them to glory in 2022.

Rinku Singh appointed Regional Sports Officer, thanks UP government for honourIndian cricketer Rinku Singh on Wednesday thanked the Uttar Pradesh Government and Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath for appointing him as a Regional Sports Officer under the sports quota.

Meet Mangesh Yadav: Son of a truck driver, now set to live IPL dream at RCBIPL 2026: Madhya Pradesh fast bowler Mangesh Yadav is set for his maiden stint with the Royal Challengers Bengaluru, who bought the pacer for Rs 5.20 crore in the mini auction last year.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
